pr.title: Match and improve hsd_3AA7, quatlib, and ftCo functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2580 matched seven functions and improved several more across ftCo_Shouldered, hsd_3AA7, and quatlib. The strongest evidence is the PR body, changed-file metadata, diff excerpt, and GALE01 bot report showing seven new 100% matches and thirteen unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Small ftCommon matching edit for ftCo_8009C744, including stack padding and restoring a ftColl_80078710 call.
Main decomp/matching target with large dispatcher/control-flow, local lifetime, volatile, and CARD operation reshaping.
evidence_refs: pr_body: new hsd_3AA7 100% matches and remaining function improvements
bot_report: hsd_3AA7 new matches and 12 hsd_3AA7 improvements

what_changed: Added new 100% matches for ftCo_8009C744, fn_803ACF30, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803B21E8, fn_803B1F78, hsd_803B2374, and fn_803AC168.
Improved MatToQuat and multiple hsd_3AA7 functions, with the PR body specifically naming loop bounds, dispatcher/control-flow structure, builder write paths, and local value lifetimes.
In ftCo_8009C744, replaced a zeroed dummy stack array with PAD_STACK(8), called ftCo_800DC920 through fp->victim_gobj, and restored ftColl_80078710(vic_gobj, gobj, &fp->dmg.facing_dir_1).

In hsd_803AAA48, reshaped command dispatch around cmd->type, explicit switch cases, direct cmd->state access, repeated retry loops for CARD APIs, and explicit command completion by setting cmd->type = 0.
Marked hsd_804D7980 as volatile in hsd_3AA7.c.
Bot report recorded total matched code movement of +0.06% and +2332 bytes for GALE01.
smart_moves: Used narrow verification reported in the PR body: python configure.py --require-protos, ninja, and GALE01 report evidence for claimed 100% functions.
Kept the ftCo_Shouldered edit minimal while converting the function to 100%, limiting the changed hunk to stack shape and call restoration.
For hsd_3AA7, concentrated most churn in one file and one subsystem rather than mixing unrelated source areas.
Made dispatcher structure more explicit by reading cmd->type and switching on it, which correlates with a large hsd_803AAA48 score improvement in the bot report.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching large async dispatchers, case ordering, the exact discriminant expression, and whether state is cached in a local versus reloaded through cmd->state can materially affect MWCC output.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_803AAA48 changes type = entry->x10/state local to type = cmd->type and repeated cmd->state

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fighter-function matches may require restoring real helper calls before damage/knockback updates rather than leaving commented-out calls or placeholder stack arrays.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCo_8009C744 replaces commented ftColl_80078710 with call using vic_gobj, gobj, and facing_dir_1

decomp_lessons.lesson: Volatile qualification on globals involved in dispatcher or interrupt-adjacent state can be part of matching source shape.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_804D7980 changed from extern s32 to extern volatile s32
diff: hsd_803AAA48 uses OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts around busy state

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven hsd_3AA7 functions kept address-style names such as fn_803ACF30 and hsd_803B2374; the PR did not introduce speculative semantic names for these symbols.
Local fighter variables use established short role names such as fp, vic_gobj, vic_fp, pos, and hit where roles are clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(8) replaced a dummy u8 stack array in ftCo_8009C744.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCo_8009C744 removes u8 _[12] = { 0 } and adds PAD_STACK(8)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is a narrow tactic with GALE01 match evidence for the function, but no inline rationale explains why PAD_STACK(8) was the only viable source shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expanded boolean assignment for busy state instead of relying on a comparison expression.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_803AAA48 changes s32 busy = hsd_804D799C == 1 to explicit if/else assigning 1 or 0
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported as a local matching tactic only; do not generalize without more examples.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Reordered and expanded switch cases in hsd_803AAA48 around CARD read/write/open/close retry loops.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_803AAA48 switch ((u32) type) and cases 5, 11, 10, 6, 2, 15, 4, 1, 16 excerpt
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff excerpt is truncated, so the full dispatcher transformation is only partially visible in loaded evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed local value lifetimes by removing a cached CardState* local in visible hsd_803AAA48 code and repeatedly accessing or casting through cmd->state/op->x4.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_803AAA48 removes CardState* state and CardState* st locals in visible hunk
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Useful as a search clue for similar hsd_3AA7 matches, not a general style recommendation.

Some lessons are local matching observations rather than accepted global standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2580 hsd_3AA7 dispatcher matching tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2580, hsd_803AAA48 improved from 28.60% to 77.68% after reshaping command dispatch around cmd->type, explicit switch cases, direct cmd->state access, CARD retry loops, and local lifetime changes.
evidence_refs: pr_body: dispatcher/control-flow structure and local value lifetimes

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2580 ftCo_8009C744 PAD_STACK and helper-call restoration
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2580, ftCo_8009C744 reached 100% after replacing a dummy stack array with PAD_STACK(8), calling ftCo_800DC920 via fp->victim_gobj, and restoring ftColl_80078710(vic_gobj, gobj, &fp->dmg.facing_dir_1).

source_update_candidates.title: hsd_3AA7.c contains CARD async dispatcher matching work
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2580 provides evidence that src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c includes CARD operation dispatcher code involving cmd->type, cmd->state, CARDFastOpen, CARDReadAsync, CARDWriteAsync, CARDClose, CARDGetXferredBytes, and interrupt state globals.
evidence_refs: diff: hsd_803AAA48 visible CARD operation cases
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path fact for future hsd_3AA7 matching searches; should be curated as source-owned path metadata, not inferred as a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK use should retain narrow verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2580 contains a narrow PAD_STACK(8) use in ftCo_8009C744 with GALE01 100% evidence. This supports the existing standard that stack padding tactics need explicit match evidence, but does not by itself justify a new broad PAD_STACK rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible example for an existing standards note; curator should decide whether to add as an example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the recorded approval; no inline review feedback was loaded.
Do not generalize hsd_803AAA48's repeated cmd->state access or explicit busy if/else into project-wide style; evidence only supports local matching usefulness.
Do not claim MatToQuat reached 100%; bot report shows 99.92%.
Do not claim full hsd_3AA7 dispatcher reconstruction details from the truncated diff excerpt.
